instant.description: Micro-feedback: focus rings, validation flashes, press states. Existing uses: sds-input-focus, sds-input-ink.

fast.description: Quick entrances and snappy UI moves. Identical to the sds-fast modifier (0.4s).

base.description: Default entrance duration. Identical to the legacy --sds-duration default (0.8s) — the most-used entrance duration in the library (73 direct/indirect uses).

slow.description: Deliberate, cinematic entrances. Identical to the sds-slow modifier (1.4s); 15 direct uses.

slower.description: Hero-scale reveals. Identical to the sds-xslow modifier (2.2s).

dramatic.description: Ambient loops and atmosphere (glow, aurora, drift). 30 direct uses of 3s in v4.0.3.

standard.description: The SDS signature ease-out (legacy --sds-easing default, 100+ uses). Confident deceleration with a soft landing. Use for most entrances.

decelerate.description: Expo-style deceleration — starts very fast, lands very softly. Use for large translations and streaks (liquid-rise, streak-in, slide-blur).

accelerate.description: Anticipate-then-accelerate (back-in). Element winds up slightly, then commits. Use for exits and weight-drop style falls.

emphasized.description: Cubic ease-out used by the multi-bounce choreography family (bounce-in-up, gravity-bounce, drop-bounce). Strong start, controlled settle.

spring.description: Back-out overshoot (~6%). The library's signature 'pop' — use for scale/pop entrances and spring settles (12 uses).

bounce.description: Anticipation + overshoot on both ends. Use for playful gravity effects (gravity-drop, card-snap; 8 uses).

sm.description: Subtle entrance offset — text lines, list items.

lg.description: Dramatic entrance offset — heroes, full-bleed media.
